subject: Physics
title: How Electric Motors Work
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on How Electric Motors Work.

hook.text: An electric motor turns electricity into motion using the force between a current-carrying wire and a magnet.

buildup.text: When current flows through a wire in a magnetic field, the wire experiences a force—it pushes sideways.

discovery.text: Coil the wire into a loop inside a magnet and the force creates rotation—a spinning motor.

twist.text: A motor is just a generator running backward. Feed in electricity and get motion. Feed in motion and get electricity.

climax.text: Electric motors power everything from fans to electric cars to the International Space Station's systems.

punchline.text: Current meets magnet. Wire spins. That's every electric motor ever built.

quiz.question: What is the relationship between an electric motor and a generator?
options: They're the same device run in opposite directions
They use completely different physical principles
Motors use magnets but generators don't
